active.directive: Homepage hero is unreadable on both desktop and mobile (white/cyan text on pale gray + glow blur). Fix contrast to WCAG-compliant levels, remove the haze effect, and tighten spacing so core value proposition is legible within 2 seconds.

Top navigation ships broken IA: 'Οδηγοί' and 'Επαγγελματίες' currently return 404 from the live menu. Point menu items to real routes (or publish those pages) and add an automated test that fails deploys on nav links returning non-200.

Service taxonomy is inconsistent and exposes dead pages: category route '/thermansi' returns 404 while Heating appears as a promoted service path. Align slugs across homepage, footer, and category data so every promoted category resolves to a valid landing page.

Category listing cards look synthetic and untrustworthy (repetitive placeholder-like provider names, duplicated patterns, no real credibility signals). Replace fake-looking entries with validated marketplace data: consistent naming, service area clarity, review count provenance, and realistic profile snippets.

Lead forms on category pages lack basic trust/compliance UX: no explicit privacy consent checkbox, no clear personal-data notice near submit, and no expectation-setting after submission. Add GDPR-compliant consent text + policy link, and show clear success/failure states with response time expectations.

history.directive: Focus priorities: (1) continuously test and improve the site (functionality, features, look & feel), (2) continuously find new worker leads, (3) prioritize top-notch SEO across the site.
Stop the MVP admin dashboard work. Founder does not want it built.
Add a roadmap item for a leads page where leads can be viewed by category (and related filters).
Bump Leads Directory Page priority to top and implement immediately.

Professional pages are shipping broken image URLs (Unsplash 404s), e.g. /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos and /professionals/maria-georgiou-ydravlikos. Replace dead image sources immediately and add image URL validation in build so no profile card can publish with a broken hero/avatar.

Professional profile UX is unacceptable: pages show failure-state copy to users (e.g. 'Αξιοπιστία: προσωρινά χαμηλή λόγω αποτυχίας φόρτωσης', 'Σφάλμα φόρτωσης κριτικών'). Hide internal failure diagnostics from public UI, implement graceful fallback for reviews, and show a clean empty-state instead of panic messaging.

Homepage and key landing pages are missing social preview essentials (no og:image, no twitter:card). Add complete Open Graph + Twitter metadata with a real branded image so links don’t look broken/cheap when shared.

The 404 experience is broken for SEO and trust: typo URLs like /profissionals return a page without title/description. Ship a proper custom 404 with meaningful <title>, meta description, clear navigation back to categories/home, and index control.

City service pages are near-clones (e.g. /episkevi-klimatistikou-athina vs /episkevi-klimatistikou-thessaloniki are ~96% identical text). Rewrite with real local differentiation (neighborhood coverage, local pricing realities, local proof/reviews) or you’re creating doorway-style thin pages that look programmatic and low-trust.

Σελίδα /professionals: η 3η κάρτα επαγγελματία (Γιάννης Κώστα) εμφανίζεται χωρίς φωτογραφία και αφήνει άδειο λευκό block στην κορυφή. Διόρθωσε το source της εικόνας και βάλε αξιόπιστο fallback image + fixed aspect ratio ώστε να μη διαλύεται οπτικά η κάρτα.

Σελίδα /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos: η κεντρική φωτογραφία προφίλ είναι broken (φαίνεται alt text αντί για εικόνα). Αυτό σκοτώνει εμπιστοσύνη. Διόρθωσε asset path/CDN και πρόσθεσε server-side validation ώστε να μην δημοσιεύεται προφίλ με νεκρή εικόνα.

Σελίδα /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos: στο section 'Αξιολογήσεις' εμφανίζεται κόκκινο μήνυμα σφάλματος validation πριν ο χρήστης κάνει οποιαδήποτε ενέργεια. Μην δείχνεις errors από προεπιλογή — ενεργοποίησε validation μόνο μετά από submit/interaction.

Homepage (mobile): το badge 'PREMIUM MARKETPLACE FOR HOME SERVICES' είναι στα αγγλικά ενώ όλη η σελίδα είναι στα ελληνικά. Αυτό δείχνει πρόχειρο template και όχι ελληνικό brand. Κάνε πλήρη γλωσσική ενοποίηση στα ελληνικά ή βάλε language toggle με συνεπή copy strategy.

Homepage: το page length είναι υπερβολικό (τεράστιο endless list υπηρεσιών) και θυμίζει dump περιεχομένου αντί για marketplace landing. Σπάσε την πληροφορία με ισχυρά φίλτρα, pagination/accordion ανά κατηγορία και lazy loading για να μειωθεί το αρχικό βάρος και το scroll fatigue.
The site still feels templated. Research and execute a 10x upgrade to make it world-class with a unique brand identity. This is now the #1 priority.

Homepage canonical is wrong/inconsistent: / returns canonical http://100.95.148.70:4322/ while category pages canonicalize to https://greekhandy.gr/... . Pick one public host (production domain) and enforce it everywhere, plus add 301 redirects from IP host to canonical domain to avoid duplicate-indexing.

HTTP response headers are missing charset (content-type is just text/html). Greek text can degrade into mojibake in some clients/crawlers. Serve text/html; charset=utf-8 globally and verify decoding consistency on all templates.

High-intent public routes are hard-404ing right now: /blog and /epikoinonia. Either ship real pages immediately or remove all references/sitemap entries and 301 them to relevant live destinations. Dead-end pages kill trust.

Slug strategy is inconsistent and creates avoidable 404s: /katharismos fails while /katharismoi exists. Implement redirect aliases for singular/plural Greek category variants (and common transliteration variants) instead of punishing users for natural URL guesses.

Homepage ships an extreme HTML payload (~2.8MB, ~447 internal links in initial markup) that looks programmatic and bloated. Split long service/city lists behind progressive disclosure (accordion + pagination/load-more) and trim above-the-fold DOM so first load feels like a real product, not an SEO dump.
Homepage is endless scrolling and feels like an SEO dump. Redesign homepage information architecture to reduce scroll fatigue and improve UX while preserving SEO quality.

Homepage hero contrast is unacceptable (especially on mobile): headline/body text blends into the pale background and is barely readable. Rework hero typography/colors to pass WCAG contrast and remove the washed-out gradient aesthetic that looks broken.

A floating accessibility toolbar is rendering on top of content (seen across category/profile/blog pages), covering CTAs and text. Dock it unobtrusively or collapse it to a small trigger so it never blocks core UI.

/blog is still a thin placeholder ('coming soon' style) with no real articles, which looks unfinished and low-trust. Publish actual Greek guides immediately (with author/date/reading time) or remove blog from primary nav until content exists.

Category pages like /ydravlikoi are bloated with an endless grid of low-context service cards. Add meaningful grouping + filters (urgency, city, budget) and reduce initial card dump so users can choose services without fatigue.

Public forms are functionally broken: POST to /api/professionals/register, /api/reviews/submit, /api/bookings/submit, and /api/messages/submit returns HTTP 500 with a TypeError page. Replace crash behavior with proper server-side validation + user-safe error responses (4xx with inline field errors), and prove each form can submit without exposing stack traces.

You are shipping a development build to users. HTML includes thousands of `data-astro-source-file`/`data-astro-source-loc` attributes with absolute server paths (e.g. /home/weakwire/projects/greekhandy/src/...). Strip all debug/source-location attributes from production output immediately; this is unprofessional leakage and massive DOM bloat.

Error responses expose Vite dev artifacts (`/@vite/client` and raw `<title>TypeError</title>`). Configure production error handling so users get clean branded error pages, no dev scripts, no framework internals, no stack-style crash output.

Core SEO trust signal is missing: key pages (/, /professionals, /ydravlikoi, /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos) have no JSON-LD structured data. Implement schema.org markup now (Organization + WebSite on homepage, Service on category pages, LocalBusiness/ProfessionalService + aggregateRating on profile pages) so search snippets don’t look empty and generic.

Homepage is still shipping as a bloated dev dump: ~3.97MB HTML, ~1,924 internal links in initial markup, and ~10,381 `data-astro-source-*` debug attributes leaked to users. Strip all source-location attrs in production and redesign homepage IA so real users are not forced through absurd SEO-scroll sludge.

Critical SEO regression: homepage `/` and professional profile `/professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos` render without `<title>` and without meta description. Add unique, human-readable title + description per template immediately or search snippets will look broken/empty.

Greek copy is rendering as mojibake (garbled characters) on live pages, especially mobile homepage and profile views. Stop shipping unreadable text: enforce UTF-8 end-to-end (headers + templates + assets) and add a deployment check that fails build when Greek strings are mis-encoded.

Professional profile contact flow is still broken: forms post to `/api/bookings/submit` and `/api/messages/submit`, but both endpoints return 404. Wire these routes to real handlers (or change form actions) and confirm successful submission UX instead of dead POSTs.

/professionals still shows a broken image card for Γιάννης Κώστα (blank hero with alt text), and his profile hero image also fails. This is a direct trust killer. Fix asset pipeline/CDN references and block publish when profile media cannot be fetched.

Mobile homepage hero is still unreadable: light cyan/white text on a pale background fails contrast and looks visually broken. Redesign the hero color system (background, heading, subtext, CTAs) to pass WCAG AA on real devices, not just desktop mocks.

/professionals and /professionals/giannis-kosta-klimatismos are still shipping broken profile media (blank image block with alt text). This kills trust instantly. Fix media URLs, add hard fallback images, and block publishing any professional profile that fails image fetch validation.

A floating accessibility widget is still sitting on top of core content across listing/category/profile pages. Reposition it to a non-blocking docked trigger (or auto-hide) so it never covers copy, buttons, or forms.

Category pages (/ydravlikoi, /ilektrologoi) still dump a huge repetitive card wall with weak differentiation and no practical filtering. Add real decision controls (city, urgency, budget, availability) and reduce initial card load so users can find a service without endless scrolling.

Professional profile UX is confused and bloated: users are hit with multiple competing forms in one page (quick contact, booking request, direct message), which creates friction and distrust. Consolidate into one primary conversion flow with a clear CTA hierarchy and move secondary contact options behind toggles.
